content.question: Which specific institutional features (funding equalization, teacher professionalism requirements, curriculum mandates, nearby-school assignment defaults, anti-segregation policies) were most critical to containing segregation growth in Finland after its 1970s comprehensive reform, and which of these can Hungary realistically implement given its fiscal and capacity constraints?
content.reduction_path: Conduct structured qualitative comparison of institutional guardrails present in Finland (1970s-2000s) versus Hungary (current), including detailed fiscal analysis of regional funding variation, teacher labor-market data, and municipal capacity assessments, paired with interviews of Hungarian education policymakers on feasibility of each guardrail.
